# The AI second brain: The future of knowledge work

Published in TechRadar Pro, June 22, 2026.

Full article: https://www.techradar.com/pro/the-ai-second-brain-the-future-of-knowledge-work

## Summary

A mainstream accessible version of the 80/20 + second brain thesis written for a general enterprise audience. Most companies use AI at the wrong level—at the output layer (emails, documents, meetings)—rather than the invisible thinking layer where 80% of knowledge work actually happens. The practical model: a "second brain" or AI context vault that gives AI persistent, compounding context about you and your work.

## Key arguments

- **AI is aimed at the wrong place:** Workplace AI is deployed into visible outputs. That 20% matters, but it's too late in the process to transform how work is done.
- **The real opportunity is the invisible 80%:** Thinking, processing, judging, planning—it happens in workers' heads, unseen. AI can reach this layer today, with current models and tools.
- **Software developers figured this out first:** Use AI not as a one-off tool but as something that builds context over time. That's the model.
- **Practical steps to build a second brain:**
  1. Move beyond web-based interfaces where every conversation starts over
  2. Create a centralized repository and give AI access to your files
  3. Have AI interview you about your work style, priorities, preferences
  4. Review and refine until AI has a real working understanding of you
  5. Build on each session—a continued process, not a one-time exercise
- **Enterprise tradeoffs:** Classic trust/hallucination questions still apply. Visibility of "back-and-forth" work decreases. Workers take their context vault when they leave. Enterprise AI pricing is a real cost (thousands of dollars of tokens per month for heavy users).

## Audience

Written for a mainstream enterprise technology audience—not practitioners, not researchers. The accessible gateway version of the full second brain thesis.

## Frameworks used

- The invisible 80% of knowledge work
- Second brain / AI context vault
- Use AI like a developer (persistent context, not one-shot)
